manufacturers.name: Lapua
manufacturers.notes: Reference-grade brass for precision shooting; consistent weight and capacity, widely used in PRS and benchrest. Excellent neck consistency and long case life.

manufacturers.name: ADG / Atlas Development Group
manufacturers.notes: Made in USA; tight tolerances, weight-sorted, annealed necks. Popular in long-range and ELR competition. Strong magnum and wildcat offerings.

manufacturers.name: Peterson Cartridge
manufacturers.notes: Pittsburgh-based; small primer pocket variants on .308 and .30-06, plus annealed necks. Strong long-action and magnum lineup.

manufacturers.name: Alpha Munitions
manufacturers.notes: OCD (Optimized Case Design) with small rifle primer pockets on most SKUs. Tight weight tolerance (typically +/- 0.5 grain). Favored in PRS and F-Class.

manufacturers.name: Capstone / Berger
manufacturers.notes: Berger-branded brass produced by Capstone Precision Group; small-batch premium for match shooters.

manufacturers.name: Hornady
manufacturers.notes: Widely available match-grade brass; pioneer of Creedmoor and PRC families. Good consistency for the price; bulk packaging.

manufacturers.name: Norma
manufacturers.notes: European premium brass; strong lineup in classic European calibers and dangerous game cartridges.

manufacturers.name: Sako
manufacturers.notes: Premium Finnish brass; smaller catalog than Lapua but high quality.

manufacturers.name: Nosler
manufacturers.notes: Weighed, deburred, neck-sized, and chamfered before packaging. Proprietary Nosler cartridge family.

manufacturers.name: Federal
manufacturers.notes: American Eagle and Premium Gold Medal lines. Gold Medal Match brass is well regarded.

manufacturers.name: Winchester
manufacturers.notes: Widely available unprimed brass; Winchester WSM family of magnums.

manufacturers.name: Remington
manufacturers.notes: Domestic standard brass; UMC and Remington-branded.

manufacturers.name: Starline
manufacturers.notes: The gold standard for handgun brass and obsolete/cowboy action calibers.

manufacturers.name: Top Brass
manufacturers.notes: Reconditioned once-fired brass; cleaned, polished, sized, and inspected. Mostly mil-surplus headstamps.

manufacturers.name: IMI / Israel Military Industries
manufacturers.notes: Military-spec brass; IMI/IWI headstamp. Strong heads, often crimped primer pockets.

manufacturers.name: Prvi Partizan / PPU
manufacturers.notes: Excellent source for obscure military and European cartridges. Affordable, often the only new-production brass for old surplus calibers.

manufacturers.name: Sellier & Bellot
manufacturers.notes: Long-established European maker (since 1825); good selection of European hunting and military calibers.

manufacturers.name: Weatherby
manufacturers.notes: Weatherby-branded brass produced by Norma in Sweden. Required for proprietary Weatherby Magnum cartridges.
